title: Recurrent otitis media history

investigations.text: Supports concerns and functional impact, hearing difficulty, speech or behaviour context, balance, URTI and AOM history, mouth breathing, adenoid context, otoscopy, developmental assessment, hearing testing, and tympanometry.
investigations.rationale: The exact paediatric OME section supports clinician-performed ENT assessment and review of hearing or tympanometry results when recurrent AOM history raises hearing or effusion concern; it does not diagnose OME or recommend tubes. The exact paediatric OME section supports reviewing AOM history, hearing, language, balance, otoscopy, hearing testing, and tympanometry; no broad legacy mapping was added because the workflow lacks age and OME qualifiers.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports concerns and functional impact, hearing difficulty, speech or behaviour context, balance, URTI and AOM history, mouth breathing, adenoid context, otoscopy, developmental assessment, hearing testing, and tympanometry.

source.title: Otitis media with effusion in under 12s — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.2.7 — concerns, impact, recognition, and formal assessment

treatment_and_management.text: Supports clinician-arranged reassessment, interval hearing impact, changing needs, and documented questions without generating an interval or intervention.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact paediatric reassessment section supports a clinician-entered plan, safety net, arranged follow-up, and questions only when documented; it does not set an interval or treatment.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-arranged reassessment, interval hearing impact, changing needs, and documented questions without generating an interval or intervention.

source.title: Otitis media with effusion in under 12s — Recommendations

follow_up.text: Defines the pediatric acute-otitis-media population, usual duration, reassessment triggers, otorrhoea context, and serious-illness review.
follow_up.rationale: The exact paediatric AOM section supports episode timing, current symptom impact, associated features, relevant negatives, rapid worsening or systemic context, and clinician reassessment without diagnosing the current episode.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Defines the pediatric acute-otitis-media population, usual duration, reassessment triggers, otorrhoea context, and serious-illness review.

sources_used.title: Otitis media with effusion in under 12s — Recommendations
sources_used.jurisdiction: England, United Kingdom; international evidence requiring UAE paediatric, audiology, and ENT adaptation

source_limitations: Age, episode count and dates, diagnostic certainty, laterality, fever, pain, discharge, hearing, speech, risk factors, examination, audiology, treatment history, resistance, procedure, and referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
